posts.author: shay.butran (shay butran)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey,
I connect to Monday’s server through a tunnel connection from my computer in VS CODE.
Everything was working normally until yesterday when Monday’s server suddenly stopped responding.
What can I do?
When I try to enter the address in the browser - https://XXXXXXXXX.apps-tunnel.monday.app/health
I get the following message:

image989×324 15.7 KB

I’m sure my service functioning ok and the problem is in the Monday server.
What can I do?
I opened a ticket yesterday but nothing happened unfortunately.

posts.author: Fario_Consulting (Fario Consulting)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi,
Try using ngrok as an alternative to mondayTunnel for your tunnel connection and see if it solves the problem.

posts.author: davidmiller (David Millar)
posts.cookedHtml:
shay butran:

Hey,
I connect to Monday’s server through a tunnel connection from my computer in VS CODE.
Everything was working normally until yesterday when Monday’s server suddenly stopped responding.
What can I do?

Check your tunnel connection settings and ensure there are no network issues. Restart VS Code and try reconnecting to the server. Verify if Monday’s server is experiencing downtime or maintenance. Contact Monday’s support for further assistance.

posts.author: Miccyox (Miccyox)
posts.cookedHtml: I’ve had that happen before, and it’s never fun when the server stops responding out of nowhere. The first thing I’d suggest is to check if there were any server outages or maintenance updates from your hosting provider.

posts.author: KaiVias (KaiVias)
posts.cookedHtml: Suppose you’re sure your service is satisfactory. In that case, it might help to double-check the basics—try clearing your browser cache, restarting VS Code, and testing the connection on another network or device. You could also try a different browser or tool to see if it’s isolated to one setup. Since you’ve already opened a ticket, maybe give it a bump or reach out on their community forum for faster attention.

posts.author: Miccyox (Miccyox)
posts.cookedHtml:
I’ve had that happen before, and it’s never fun when the server stops responding out of nowhere. The first thing I’d suggest is to check if there were any server outages or maintenance updates from your hosting provider. Sometimes, they perform updates without giving a heads-up. You can also try restarting the server to see if that does the trick. It might be worth contacting your host’s support team to get more info if it’s still down.
If you’re looking for a reliable VPS provider with good uptime, I’ve been using https://ishosting.com/en/dedicated for a while now, and they’ve been solid for me. They have good support and offer fast response times when issues pop up.
